text.title: K8s VPA Rightsizing Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that govern the VPA-recommend → HPA-reconcile → rightsizing rollout. These prevent the most common failures: eviction storms and HPA conflicts.

rule.statement: Always start VPA in `updateMode: "Off"` (recommend-only). Never enable `updateMode: "Auto"` on production workloads with HPA attached. Reject any plan that skips recommend mode.
rule.rationale: VPA Auto mode and HPA on the same workload create scaling oscillation and pod eviction storms. Recommend mode produces the numbers; humans apply.

rule.statement: Soak VPA recommendations for ≥ 7 days BEFORE acting on them. Reject decisions on recommendations < 7 days old. Production workload patterns include weekly cycles invisible in shorter windows.
rule.rationale: Weekly business cycles (Mon vs Sun, daily vs nightly batches) only show in ≥ 7-day windows. Shorter soak undersizes workloads at peak.

rule.statement: When applying new per-pod sizing, recompute HPA min/max replicas so the total cluster headroom is preserved. If new per-pod CPU goes from 500m to 200m and HPA min was 3 (1500m headroom), new HPA min should be ≥ 8 (1600m headroom). Reject sizing changes without HPA reconciliation.
rule.rationale: Smaller pods + same HPA min = less aggregate headroom = traffic spike causes scaling lag = SLO breach. Reconciliation maintains effective capacity.

rule.statement: Apply rightsizing to a 10% canary cohort first (single AZ or labeled subset). Soak canary ≥ 48 hours. Promote to full rollout ONLY when OOMKill rate, CPU throttle rate, and SLO error budget all stay within pre-canary baseline +/- 10%. Reject promotions skipping canary or with worse-than-baseline metrics.
rule.rationale: Canary catches workload-specific resource misjudgments before the full cluster is affected. 10% is large enough to surface real load patterns.

rule.statement: If ANY canary workload OOMKills > 1× per pod per 24h during canary window, ROLLBACK that workload's sizing. Document under-recommended workloads as exceptions and revisit after additional soak. No "let it ride" on OOMKills.
rule.rationale: OOMKills propagate via cascade restarts and cache loss. Even single events in canary predict cluster-wide instability if promoted as-is.

example.title: Reconciled sizing change with HPA envelope
example.code: Workload: api-svc Before: requests: cpu=500m mem=512Mi; HPA min=3 max=20 VPA reco: target: cpu=200m mem=384Mi (soaked 9 days) After: requests: cpu=200m mem=384Mi; HPA min=8 max=50 (per-pod CPU ÷ 2.5 → min × 2.6 to preserve headroom) Canary: 10% (1 AZ) — 48h soak, OOMKills=0, throttle within +3%, SLO green Promotion: approved 2026-05-19 Savings: -38% CPU requests cluster-wide (~$430/mo)
example.note: Illustrates r1-r5 in a successful rollout.

example.title: Auto mode + HPA mismatch
example.code:
Engineer enables VPA updateMode: Auto on workload with HPA min=3, max=20.
Within 6 hours: - VPA evicts pods to apply new requests. - HPA sees CPU spike during pod restarts, scales to max=20. - VPA flags new pods as over-provisioned, recommends downsize. - Cycle repeats. Cluster pods restart 300+ times in 24h.
example.note: Illustrates r1 violation — the most common VPA failure mode.
